skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Applies Anthropic-inspired brand colors, typography choices, and visual styling guidance to documents, presentations, and other design artifacts.
skill.summary: Apply Anthropic-inspired colors and typography to visual artifacts.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported URLs, weak cryptography keywords, reconnaissance keywords, and shell backticks. Review found these are false positives from Apache License text, Markdown inline color values, and ordinary brand guidance; no executable code, network calls, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: License URLs Misclassified as Network Activity
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: LICENSE.txt contains standard Apache License reference URLs. These are static legal references and do not perform network requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are part of standard Apache License text. No executable network API, script, or command is present near these URLs.

low_findings.title: Apache License Terms Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static high-severity weak cryptography alerts occur inside Apache License prose, including contribution, notice, trademark, warranty, and appendix language. No cryptographic API or algorithm selection is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are legal text, not code. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic usage in an executable context.

low_findings.title: Markdown Color Values Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: SKILL.md uses Markdown backticks around hexadecimal color values. These are documentation literals, not Ruby or shell command substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks only format color constants in Markdown. No script block, interpreter directive, or command execution primitive is present.

low_findings.title: Brand Guidance Text Misclassified as Blocker Patterns
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static alerts on SKILL.md point to description, keyword, font fallback, color, and RGBColor documentation. The reviewed text does not instruct reconnaissance, system inspection, weak cryptography, or prompt override behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is normal design documentation. No evidence found of system probing, network probing, malicious intent, or instructions to bypass the audit.

content.user_title: Apply Brand Styling to Visual Work
content.value_statement: Teams need consistent visual styling across documents, slides, and design artifacts. This skill provides color, typography, and formatting guidance for Anthropic-inspired presentation work.
actual_capabilities: Lists primary, neutral, and accent colors for visual styling.
Recommends Poppins for headings and Lora for body text.
Defines fallback fonts for systems without custom fonts installed.
Guides smart color selection for text, shapes, and backgrounds.
Describes how to preserve hierarchy and readability while applying style.
limitations: Does not include executable styling scripts or automation code.
Does not install fonts or verify local font availability.
Does not include official brand assets, logos, or image files.
Does not validate trademark or legal permission for brand use.

use_cases.title: Style a Presentation
use_cases.description: Apply consistent colors, headings, body fonts, and accent shapes to a slide deck.

use_cases.title: Format Internal Documentation
use_cases.description: Use the documented palette and typography rules to make reports visually consistent.

use_cases.title: Review Design Consistency
use_cases.description: Check whether a visual artifact follows the stated colors, font hierarchy, and readability guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Basic Styling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple document or slide.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this skill's brand colors and typography guidance to my artifact. Keep the layout readable and preserve the original content.

prompt_templates.title: Restyle a Slide Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Restyle this presentation using the documented color palette, heading font, body font, and accent colors. Explain the visible changes briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Visual Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review before sharing a design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this artifact against the skill's brand guidance. Identify color, typography, hierarchy, and readability issues.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Detailed Style Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a detailed styling plan for slides, handouts, and web mockups using this skill's palette, typography, and fallback rules.

output_examples.input: Restyle a product update slide using the brand guidance.
output_examples.output: The slide uses dark text on a light background, Poppins-style headings, Lora-style body copy, and orange accents for primary emphasis.

output_examples.input: Review this report cover for consistency.
output: The heading hierarchy is clear and follows the recommended heading treatment.
The body text should use the documented serif fallback for better consistency.
The accent shape should use orange, blue, or green instead of an unrelated color.

output_examples.input: Prepare a style plan for a short deck.
output_examples.output: Use light backgrounds for content slides, dark backgrounds for section breaks, and rotate the three accent colors across supporting shapes.

best_practices: Provide the artifact format and intended audience before requesting styling changes.
Keep original content intact unless the prompt asks for rewriting.
Confirm font availability when exact typography is important.
anti_patterns: Do not use the guidance as proof of official brand approval.
Do not apply accent colors so heavily that readability suffers.
Do not assume the skill includes logos, templates, or licensed assets.

faq.question: Does this skill contain executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files contain Markdown guidance and license text only.

faq.question: Can it install Poppins or Lora fonts?
faq.answer: No. It recommends fonts and fallbacks, but it does not install software or font files.

faq.question: Does this provide official legal permission to use a brand?
faq.answer: No. Users must confirm trademark and brand permissions separately.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use this guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is documented for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.

faq.question: What artifacts work best with this skill?
faq.answer: Presentations, reports, handouts, and visual drafts benefit most from the color and typography guidance.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level low?
faq.answer: Static alerts were reviewed as false positives, but the community source and brand-permission limits still merit caution.
